title: Internal error in static function if a loop variable is initialized in the loop
body:
The following leads to an internal error:

```sv
module m;
function static void f();
        for (int i=0; i<1; ++i) begin
                int v = i;
        end
endfunction
endmodule
```

```
%Error: Internal Error: m.sv:4:11: ../V3Scope.cpp:80: Can't locate varref scope
-node: VARREF 0x5555564bfd80 <e829> {d4al} @dt=0x5555564d44a0@(G/sw32)  unnamedblk1__DOT__i [RV] <- VAR 0x5555564d4570 <e903> {d3al} @dt=0x5555564d44a0@(G/sw32)  unnamedblk1__DOT__i [LOOP] [FUNC] [VAUTOM]  VAR
    4 |   int v = i;
      |           ^
```

Removing the declaration from the for loop solves the issue:

```sv
module m;
function static void f();
        int i;
        for (i=0; i<1; ++i) begin
                int v = i;
        end
endfunction
endmodule
```


---

I tested also on Questa 2021.3. Questa does not even allow initializing a static variable as follows. But this may be idiosyncratic to Questa.

```sv
module m;
function static void f();
  int a = 0;
endfunction
endmodule
```
---
I tested on the master. Error is related to b56e7f6 which adds support for static function variables.
